location.office_time_required: occasionally required for proximal roles; majority are fully remote

level_signals: Base pay range $169K-$240K depending on location, consistent with Senior PM
Equity Grade 8, Pay Grade N signal senior individual contributor

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years product management experience owning financial platforms or data products — responsible for roadmap, APIs, data models, and cross-functional delivery.

Practical accounting experience required: hands-on accounting experience with direct ownership of month-end close, reconciliations, and external audits. CPA preferred. Practical domain expertise is more important than the credential alone.
Proven track record of building and working knowledge of financial systems, subledger → GL flows, journal-entry design, reconciliations, SOX controls, and external audit processes.
Strong technical fluency — data models, ETL/DBT, SQL, and API design — and the ability to partner tightly with engineering and data teams on lineage and transformations.

Exceptional communicator and cross-functional influencer: translates accounting requirements into technical specs, drives consensus across Finance & Engineering, and presents tradeoffs to senior leaders.
Willingness to work East Coast hours
Bachelor's or Master's in Accounting, Finance, Computer Science, or other related fields; or equivalent practical experience.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: CPA preferred. Practical domain expertise is more important than the credential alone.

scope.surface: Subledger and reporting infrastructure, Accounting ↔ Engineering interface, reconciliation and controls, month-end close and auditability
primary_metrics_inferred: Data quality metrics and SLAs for critical reports and journal feeds
Reconciliation automation coverage for high-volume accounts
core_responsibilities: Own the financial reporting product roadmap: subledgers, standardized accounting-event interface, journal-entry flows, GL/ERP integrations, and reconciliation tooling.
Work with Engineering to define subledger patterns and the Accounting ↔ Engineering interface: accounting-event schemas, API contracts, domain boundaries, and migration strategies.
Translate accounting policy and audit requirements into product specs, posting logic, SOX controls, and implementable engineering requirements.
Lead matching/reconciliation strategy and automation: run vendor evaluations, define recon rules and ownership.
Own data lineage, quality metrics, SLAs, monitoring, and remediation flows for critical reports and journal feeds.
Run product discovery, prioritization, and cross-functional programs.
Drive governance and change management: coordinate Accounting, Finance, Product and Engineering; own go-to-production signoffs, runbooks, and stakeholder training.

compensation.equity_form: Equity Grade 8; ESPP (employee stock purchase plan enabling purchase of Affirm shares at a discount); equity rewards mentioned as part of total compensation

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed and competitive ($169K-$240K base depending on location)
Remote-first company with broad US remote eligibility
Clear product surface and ownership area with specific technical scope
100% subsidized medical, dental, and vision coverage is a standout benefit
Role has meaningful accounting + engineering bridge — rare and interesting scope
JD explicitly states 'Practical domain expertise is more important than the credential alone' — reduces credentialism gatekeeping
red_flags: Requirement to work East Coast hours may disadvantage candidates in PT or MT time zones despite remote-first framing
Dual requirement of deep accounting experience AND strong technical fluency (SQL, ETL/DBT, API design) is a narrow and difficult combination to find
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Pay range spans a wide $50K band per tier, making it difficult to anticipate actual offer level

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and Equity Grade 8 / Pay Grade N signals)
scope.business_model (inferred from Affirm's BNPL product description)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
compensation.base_range_usd lower bound set to $169K (non-CA/WA/NY/NJ/CT floor) and upper bound to $240K (CA/WA/NY/NJ/CT ceiling) to represent full US range
ambiguities: CPA appears both as a 'preferred' credential (soft) and within a 'required' block: 'Practical accounting experience required… CPA preferred.' Treated the accounting experience as hard and CPA credential as soft (nice_to_have), consistent with JD language.
Location type: JD uses '#LI-Remote' tag and 'remote-first' language, but also mentions East Coast hours as a requirement, which effectively restricts geography. Treated as remote-us.
base_range_usd: JD states two geographic pay tiers; the JSON captures the full range ($169K-$240K) spanning both tiers; location_pay_differential flagged as true.
